title: Level 70
wikitext:
{{DISPLAYTITLE:Level 70: "Gamma Garden"}}
{{Audio|file=Level 70 ambience.mp3|align=left}}{{Foreground|File=GardenForground.gif}} {{Background|file=Backgroundofradiation.jpg}} {{Class 4e}} '''Level 70''', colloquially known as "Gamma Garden," is the 71st level of the Backrooms. ----
Description
---- {{Blurimage|file=Gardenofirradiated.jpg|caption=The radiation found within the Garden has the expected effect on cameras.}} Level 70 is characterized as a series of labyrinthine gardens and small patches of grass that all surround a highly radioactive portion of the level dubbed the '''Epicenter'''. Whilst the level lacks an entity presence, it is still incredibly dangerous due to the presence of [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gamma_ray gamma radiation] as one approaches the Epicenter, leading to radiation poisoning and death. Initially, the flowers within Level 70 are an exact match to many flowers within [[The Frontrooms]], though as one approaches the Epicenter the flowers become mutated and begin to smell more and more potent and appalling. Cobblestone pathways are commonplace within Level 70, and are one of the only surfaces that a wanderer will walk on, other than grass. Plants are planted within the soil found on either side of the pathways, it is believed these plants obtain moisture anomalously due to the lack of rain in the level. Hedges and bushes are occasionally found throughout Level 70, and it has been noted that they are found more commonly close to fountains. Occasionally, these hedges can be found creating a maze-like structure leading to the next pathway. Wanderers have noted that leaves seem to eternally descend from the sky whilst traversing Level 70, even in the lack of trees or plants with noticeable leaf-like structures. These leaves are believed to simply manifest within the sky at random intervals, creating this strange effect. Whilst the leaves themselves do not mutate, as one approaches the Epicenter they will begin to carry more and more radiation; until accidentally ingesting even just one of them can cause damage. {{Blurimage|file=Fountainbutitsanimaginedragonssong.png|caption=Fountains within Level 70 carry radiation no matter the distance from the Epicenter.|align=left}} Fountains are an occasional break from the monotony of plants in Level 70. Distributing [[Object 1|Almond Water]] from an unknown source, fountains are rarely found, with the two closest fountains ever recorded being about seven kilometers from each other. Regardless of their distance to the Epicenter, the water being distributed by fountains is always radioactive and dangerous to drink. The presence of radiation in every fountain is believed to be from the system containing the water being contaminated in one way or another. Various strange, animal-like entities have been spotted frequenting Level 70. These creatures are most commonly seen as birds, though they have been reported to occasionally take the appearance of other animals, with the most common type of animal these creatures masquerade as - other than birds - being pests and rodents. Regardless of what these creatures masquerade as, they always emit high-pitched squeaking sounds and occasional bird whistles. Plants within Level 70 were once believed to emit radiation of their own, though this has since been disproven with samples collected for [[M.E.G.]] researchers. Prior to the discovery of the Epicenter, it was believed that all of the radiation within Level 70 was from the sheer amount of the flora emitting radiation at once. This has - of course - been disproven along with the discovery of the Epicenter and that the plants do not emit gamma radiation. Numerous species of fungi found only within Level 70 parasitize the flora, causing the plant to wilt and die. These species have been noted to be heavily resistant against gamma radiation due to a layer of a strange compound across their body. Samples from these fungi have been taken out from Level 70 for study. A male [[Entity 4|deathmoth]] was coated in the compound, had a tracker inserted into him, and was sent to Level 70; where it did, indeed, have resistance to the radiation. ----
[[File:Radioactive-warning-sign-png.png|frameless|38x38px|link=]] The Epicenter [[File:Radioactive-warning-sign-png.png|frameless|38x38px|link=]]
---- {{Blurimage|file=Epicenter.jpg|caption=A recreation of what a photograph taken within the Epicenter would look like.}} The Epicenter is characterized by the sudden disappearance of any living flora, replaced by what looks like an infinite horizon of dull, dead grass. Without heavy duty equipment, stepping near the Epicenter is almost immediately fatal. Even with heavy duty equipment, a stop near the Epicenter can cause severe problems in the long run. Whilst at first glance the Epicenter appears to go on infinitely, it is no where near that. The effect of the grass seemingly going on for eternity is simply due a thick fog in the air surrounding the Epicenter preventing one from seeing the horizon. The soil of the Epicenter is reportedly incredibly unstable, one wrong step and the grass will fall beneath whoever set it off, leading to [[The Void]]. The grass falling down can only be seen by the wanderer who set it off, and if anyone was next to them all they would see is them phasing through the soil. Attempts to save falling wanderers have been proven futile, as they fall too fast into the ground. Plants nearby to the Epicenter undergo rapid mutation, some merging together and others creating completely new species. It has been reported that, in rare instances, humans can also undergo the rapid mutation process. Documentation salvaged from a burnt-down settlement states that they were bombarded by zombie-like creatures, presumably people that had underwent the rapid mutation process. People believe this as additional documentation salvaged from the rubble alleged that a large squad was sent out into the Epicenter. The Epicenter is a point of no return. If one somehow braves the expanse of dead grass, they will live to tell the tale, not for much longer. Even with the most heavy duty of equipment, it is impossible to venture to the end of the Epicenter without contracting numerous cancers at the lightest. [[M.E.G.]] has issued warnings for all of its operatives venturing through Level 70, "steer clear of the Epicenter." ----
Colonies & Outposts
----
Fountain Town
----
Named by its fountain that was disconnected from the rest of the level's water supply, Fountain Town is a [[Backroom Colonists]] outpost established on the outskirts of Level 70. Untouched by the radiation of the Epicenter, Fountain Town is open to wanderers in need of shelter from the Epicenter's radiation and has a free [[Object 1|Almond Water]] supply for all.
----
Flora Mutations
---- The following are the most common mutations found in the flora of Level 70. |-|Fasciation= {{Blurimage|file=Mutation.jpg|caption=A side-by-side comparison of an unaffected flower and a mutated one.|align=left}} [https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Fasciation Fasciation] is a phenomenon found both in [[The Frontrooms]] and Level 70. Fasciation is characterized by the flower adopting a peculiar, elongated appearance. This phenomenon can occur in any part of the flower, including the fruit. In Level 70, fasciation is caused by radiation-induced hormonal imbalances in the plant cells where growth occurs. Fasciation has additionally been noted to occur even on the outskirts of the level, where there is no radiation. When fasciation occurs on the outskirts, it is most likely due to fungal infections. |-|Trypophobic Clusters= {{Blurimage|file=Img-11.jpg|caption=A rose affected by trypophoic clusters.|align=left}} Trypophobic Clusters are characterized by heavy mutations in members of the family '''Rosaceae''', also known as the rose family. These mutations take the form of several holes at the top of the plant, with seed-like structures growing from them. Edible members of the Rosaceae family affected by Trypophobic Clusters are deemed as poisonous, and ingestion can cause numerous gut problems. It was previously theorized that plants affected by Trypophoic Clusters adopted a more fungus-like nature, reproducing via spores that would infect other plants with the mutation. This has since been debunked following the discovery of the Epicenter and the radiation within the level. Trypophobic Clusters occur closer to the Epicenter. ----
Ingress & Egress
----
Ingress
---- * *No-clipping through suspiciously vibrant flowers found in [[Level 630]] can lead either to the outskirts or Epicenter of Level 70. ----
Egress
---- *Traversing backward from the direction one found themselves looking after they no-clipped into Level 70 can lead one back to the level they came from; *Immediately no-clipping through the grass of the Epicenter as one enters it can lead to [[Level 10]]; *No-clipping through a shorter tree will take one to [[Level 118]]; *Taking a wrong step whilst walking through the Epicenter will lead one to [[The Void]].
